```{r setup, results="hide"}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = F, message = FALSE)
source("utils.R")
theme_set(theme_light())
```

#fig 6
```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(grid)

# ============================================================
# Folder config
# ============================================================

folder_path <- "output1725/result_0122/result/10000/"

file_list <- list.files(
  path = folder_path,
  pattern = "\\.rds$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

# ============================================================
# FAST extraction for multiple review metrics
# ============================================================

result_multi <- map_dfr(file_list, function(file) {

  df <- readRDS(file)
  file_name <- basename(file)

  heterogeneity_rate <- str_extract(file_name, "e[0-9.]+") %>%
    str_replace("e", "") %>%
    as.numeric()

  model_type <- str_extract(file_name, "rf|svm|nn|xgb")

  extract_metric <- function(m, metric_name) {
    idx <- which(m$metric == metric_name)
    if (length(idx) == 0) return(NA_real_)
    m$value[idx[1]]
  }

  tibble(
    file_name = file_name,
    model_type = model_type,
    heterogeneity_rate = heterogeneity_rate,

    review_pct_100 = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_100")),
    review_pct_99  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_99")),
    review_pct_95  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_95")),
    review_pct_90  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_90"))
  )
})

# ============================================================
# Long format
# ============================================================

result_long <- result_multi %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("review_pct"),
    names_to = "requirement",
    values_to = "review_pct"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    model_type = case_when(
      model_type == "rf"  ~ "Random Forest",
      model_type == "svm" ~ "SVM (RBF)",
      model_type == "nn"  ~ "Dense Neural Net",
      model_type == "xgb" ~ "XGBoost"
    ),
    requirement = case_when(
      requirement == "review_pct_100" ~ "100%",
      requirement == "review_pct_99"  ~ "99%",
      requirement == "review_pct_95"  ~ "95%",
      requirement == "review_pct_90"  ~ "90%"
    ),
    model_type = factor(
      model_type,
      levels = c(
        "Dense Neural Net",
        "SVM (RBF)",
        "Random Forest",
        "XGBoost"
      )
    ),
    requirement = factor(
      requirement,
      levels = c("100%", "99%", "95%", "90%")
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# Plot settings
# ============================================================

model_colors <- c(
  "Dense Neural Net" = "#0072B2",
  "SVM (RBF)"        = "#D55E00",
  "Random Forest"    = "#009E73",
  "XGBoost"          = "#6A3D9A"
)

line_types <- c(
  "100%" = "solid",
  "99%"  = "longdash",
  "95%"  = "dotdash",
  "90%"  = "dotted"
)

line_sizes <- c(
  "100%" = 1.6,
  "99%"  = 1.3,
  "95%"  = 1.0,
  "90%"  = 0.8
)

line_alphas <- c(
  "100%" = 1.0,
  "99%"  = 0.9,
  "95%"  = 0.8,
  "90%"  = 0.7
)

# ============================================================
# Plot
# ============================================================

p <- ggplot(
  result_long,
  aes(
    x = heterogeneity_rate,
    y = review_pct,
    color = model_type,
    linetype = requirement,
    linewidth = requirement,
    alpha = requirement
  )
) +

  geom_smooth(
    aes(fill = model_type),
    se = TRUE,
    method = "loess",
    alpha = 0.35        
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Heterogeneity Rate",
    y = "Review Percentage",
    linetype = "Review requirement\n(PPV / NPV threshold)"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = model_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types) +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = line_sizes, guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = line_alphas, guide = "none") +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 0.6, 0.1),
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ model_type, ncol = 1) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),

    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),

    legend.key.width  = unit(2.8, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    legend.spacing.y  = unit(0.4, "cm"),

    strip.text = element_text(size = 18)
  )

print(p)

# ============================================================
# Save
# ============================================================

ggsave(
  filename = paste0(
    "figures1725/fig6_",
    format(Sys.Date(), "%m%d"),
    ".png"
  ),
  plot = p,
  width = 10,
  height = 12,
  dpi = 300
)


```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Libraries
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(DT)

# ============================================================
# Folder config
# ============================================================

folder_path <- "output1725/result_0122/result/10000/"

file_list <- list.files(
  path = folder_path,
  pattern = "\\.rds$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

# ============================================================
# FAST extraction for multiple review metrics
# ============================================================

result_multi <- map_dfr(file_list, function(file) {

  df <- readRDS(file)
  file_name <- basename(file)

  heterogeneity_rate <- str_extract(file_name, "e[0-9.]+") %>%
    str_replace("e", "") %>%
    as.numeric()

  model_type <- str_extract(file_name, "rf|svm|nn|xgb")

  extract_metric <- function(m, metric_name) {
    idx <- which(m$metric == metric_name)
    if (length(idx) == 0) return(NA_real_)
    m$value[idx[1]]
  }

  tibble(
    file_name = file_name,
    model_type = model_type,
    heterogeneity_rate = heterogeneity_rate,

    review_pct_100 = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_100")),
    review_pct_99  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_99")),
    review_pct_95  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_95")),
    review_pct_90  = map_dbl(df$metrics, ~ extract_metric(.x, "review_pct_90"))
  )
})

# ============================================================
# Summary stats (Model-level, wide)
# ============================================================

summary_stats <- result_multi %>%
  group_by(model_type) %>%
  summarise(
    min_100  = min(review_pct_100, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_100 = mean(review_pct_100, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_100  = max(review_pct_100, na.rm = TRUE),

    min_99   = min(review_pct_99, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_99  = mean(review_pct_99, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_99   = max(review_pct_99, na.rm = TRUE),

    min_95   = min(review_pct_95, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_95  = mean(review_pct_95, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_95   = max(review_pct_95, na.rm = TRUE),

    min_90   = min(review_pct_90, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_90  = mean(review_pct_90, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_90   = max(review_pct_90, na.rm = TRUE),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ============================================================
# Align table with plotting logic
# (Model × Requirement × min / mean / max)
# ============================================================

summary_aligned <- summary_stats %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -model_type,
    names_to = c("stat", "requirement"),
    names_pattern = "(min|max|mean)_(100|99|95|90)",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    requirement = paste0(requirement, "%"),
    stat = factor(stat, levels = c("min", "mean", "max")),
    model_type = case_when(
      model_type == "rf"  ~ "Random Forest",
      model_type == "svm" ~ "SVM (RBF)",
      model_type == "nn"  ~ "Dense Neural Net",
      model_type == "xgb" ~ "XGBoost",
      TRUE ~ model_type
    ),
    model_type = factor(
      model_type,
      levels = c(
        "Dense Neural Net",
        "SVM (RBF)",
        "Random Forest",
        "XGBoost"
      )
    ),
    requirement = factor(
      requirement,
      levels = c("100%", "99%", "95%", "90%")
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# Final paper-ready table (wide)
# ============================================================

table_fig6 <- summary_aligned %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = stat,
    values_from = value
  ) %>%
  arrange(model_type, requirement)


table_fig6_display <- table_fig6 %>%
  mutate(
    min  = scales::percent(min,  accuracy = 0.1),
    mean = scales::percent(mean, accuracy = 0.1),
    max  = scales::percent(max,  accuracy = 0.1)
  )

datatable(
  table_fig6_display,
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(
    pageLength = 10,
    lengthMenu = c(5, 10, 20, 50),
    autoWidth = TRUE
  )
)




```
